sql_array: CREATE TABLE mail_log (id INT PRIMARY KEY, email_content TEXT, send_timestamp TIMESTAMP);

INSERT INTO mail_log (id, email_content, send_timestamp) VALUES (1, 'Subject: Test Email 1
From: <sender@example.com>
To: <recipient@example.com>

This is a test body.', NOW());

INSERT INTO mail_log (id, email_content, send_timestamp) VALUES (2, 'Subject: Test Email 2
From: <sender2@example.com>
To: <recipient2@example.com>
Cc: <cc@example.com>

This is a body for test email 2.', NOW());
SELECT * FROM mail_log WHERE id <= (SELECT MAX(id) FROM mail_log);
SELECT smtp_send('<sender@example.com>', '<recipient@example.com>'), id FROM mail_log WHERE email_content IS NOT NULL;
SELECT smtp_send('<sender@example.com>', '<recipient@example.com>') FROM mail_log WHERE id <= 2 AND send_timestamp IS NOT NULL;

SELECT smtp_send(CONCAT('<sender', id, '@example.com>'), CONCAT('<recipient', id, '@example.com>')) AS email_status FROM mail_log WHERE id <= (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mail_log) GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(email_content) >= 1;

SELECT smtp_send('Subject: Sample Subject
From: <test@example.com>
To: <target@example.com>

Body for testing

Extra Line', '<test@example.net>') FROM mail_log WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM mail_log WHERE email_content LIKE '%body%' AND id <= 3) AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mail_log) <= 10;
CREATE TABLE smtp_test (test_id INT PRIMARY KEY, message TEXT, result BOOLEAN);

INSERT INTO smtp_test (test_id, message, result) VALUES (1, 'Subject: Fuzz Test 
From: <fuzz@example.com>
To: <fuzzrecipient@example.com>

Testing with NULL case', NULL);

INSERT INTO smtp_test (test_id, message, result) VALUES (2, 'Subject: Boundary Testing
From: <boundary@example.com>
To: <boundaryrecipient@example.com>

Edge case with empty string', '');

SELECT smtp_send(CONCAT('<sender', id, '@emaildomain.com>'), '<recipient@example.com>') FROM smtp_test WHERE message IS NOT NULL AND result IS NOT NULL ORDER BY test_id WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY test_id DESC);

SELECT smtp_send('Subject: Complex Example
From: <<invalid_sender>>>
To: <recipient@example.com>

This is a message body with unexpected syntax.', '<recipient@example.com>') WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM mail_log WHERE id <= 3 AND email_content IS NOT NULL) AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mail_log) = 2;
